items.title: Latest read-only snapshot is the shared evidence anchor
items.next_step: collect a fresh read-only snapshot during the next visible outage

items.title: Current decision blocks mutation and automatic profile changes
items.next_step: keep decision=observe unless a fresh snapshot changes the failure domain

items.title: Blocking/app probe history is available as trend evidence
items.next_step: use probes as app/path evidence, not as an x-ui restart trigger

items.title: One refresh command rebuilds the local planning reports
items.next_step: run refresh after every new snapshot before deciding on action

items.title: Short incident card exists for the next outage
evidence: plain_action=VPN core is healthy. Do not restart NL; collect fresh evidence during the next visible outage.
items.next_step: start incidents from the operator card, then collect fresh evidence

items.title: Outside-in NL TCP port probe is available
items.next_step: if any public NL port fails, collect a fresh read-only snapshot and compare listeners

items.title: NL source reconciliation is locally closed and deploy-blocked
items.next_step: keep services/nl-server as reviewed local source until a separate NL write approval exists

items.title: Preflight validator passes while deploy remains blocked
items.next_step: run preflight again before any maintenance window

items.title: Future NL write is blocked until the exact approval phrase
evidence: required_phrase=approve NL write for health shell split only
items.next_step: do not stage files to NL before the exact approval phrase is given

items.title: SPB remains excluded from recovery
items.next_step: keep SPB disabled until it has its own explicit reactivation plan

items.title: Manual failover is documented but inactive
items.next_step: keep failover manual-only and require fresh evidence before any client switch

items.title: Secondary exit probe is only a safe template until a new node exists
items.next_step: choose a new non-SPB provider/region before any emergency profile test
